description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a pitched wooden roof supported by four wooden posts. The well features a visible bucket and pulley mechanism for drawing water, with the bucket hanging from a rope attached to a horizontal wooden beam. The stonework of the well is rough and textured, giving it a rustic appearance, while the wooden roof is dark and shingled, providing shelter over the open well. The structure is robust and substantial, likely intended for outdoor use in parks, courtyards, or communal urban spaces. The well's protective stone wall is high enough to prevent accidental falls, and the overall design is utilitarian with minimal ornamentation. The interior of the well appears to be open, with water visible at the bottom.
description: A rustic stone water well with a dark wooden shingled roof, supported by wooden posts and featuring a bucket and pulley mechanism. The well has a rough stone base and a functional, sturdy design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open well, bucket and pulley mechanism, and the pitched wooden roof with visible shingles.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wooden roof and the circular stone base, with the bucket visible on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the well opening and the pulley system, but with less visibility of the bucket.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back of the roof and the stone base, with the pulley mechanism more obscured.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden shingled roof supported by four posts; no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Present; a metal bucket attached to a rope and pulley system mounted on a horizontal wooden beam.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, forming a protective barrier around the well opening; wooden roof provides overhead protection.
